seo_title: Current Time in Kuwait City — AST (UTC+3)
seo_description: Current time in Kuwait City, Kuwait. Arabia Standard Time (AST, UTC+3) year-round — no DST. Live clock and time differences for Kuwait City.

faq.question: What time is it in Kuwait City right now?
faq.answer: Kuwait City, Kuwait uses Arabia Standard Time (AST, UTC+3) year-round. Kuwait does not observe Daylight Saving Time. The IANA identifier is Asia/Kuwait. Kuwait City shares its time zone with Riyadh (Saudi Arabia), Baghdad (Iraq), and Moscow (Russia). The live clock above shows the current Kuwait City time.

faq.question: What time zone is Kuwait City in?
faq.answer: Kuwait City, Kuwait is in the Arabia Standard Time zone (AST, UTC+3) year-round. The IANA identifier is Asia/Kuwait. Kuwait does not observe Daylight Saving Time, so the UTC+3 offset is constant throughout the year. Kuwait shares this time zone with Saudi Arabia, Iraq, Yemen, Bahrain, and Qatar.

faq.question: Does Kuwait City observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: No. Kuwait City and Kuwait do not observe Daylight Saving Time. Kuwait uses AST (UTC+3) permanently year-round. The entire Gulf region — including Saudi Arabia, Qatar, Bahrain, UAE, and Oman — does not observe DST.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Kuwait City and London?
faq.answer: Kuwait City (AST, UTC+3) is 3 hours ahead of London (GMT, UTC+0) in winter. During British Summer Time (BST, UTC+1), Kuwait City is 2 hours ahead of London. Kuwait does not observe DST.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Kuwait City and New York?
faq.answer: Kuwait City (AST, UTC+3) is 8 hours ahead of New York (EST, UTC-5) in winter. During US DST (EDT, UTC-4), Kuwait City is 7 hours ahead of New York. Kuwait does not observe DST.

faq.question: What are typical business hours in Kuwait City?
faq.answer: Standard business hours in Kuwait City are 8:00 AM to 4:00 PM AST, Sunday through Thursday (Friday and Saturday are the weekend). Kuwait City is the capital and largest city of Kuwait, one of the world's leading oil exporters. The Kuwait Stock Exchange (Boursa Kuwait) is one of the largest in the Arab world. Kuwait Petroleum Corporation (KPC) and its subsidiaries are major employers.
